creator: fabian@wenks.ch
text:
I did also run into this. In my case it was only roundcube which failed so far.

I have installed databases/php81-pdo_mysql with option MYSQLND, but I am not sure which dependency this would be. So I did a forced rebuild of the following ports:
databases/php81-mysqli databases/php81-pdo databases/php81-pdo_mysql

But this did not solve it, and 'pdo_mysql.default_socket' in php.ini still needs to be set to '/var/run/mysql/mysql.sock'.

Unfortunately I do not completely understand the configure stuff from databases/php81-mysqli, but when running 'make configure' the following files contain '/var/run/mysql/mysql.sock':

work/php-8.1.13/ext/mysqli/config.m4
work/php-8.1.13/ext/mysqli/autom4te.cache/output.0
work/php-8.1.13/ext/mysqli/autom4te.cache/output.1
work/php-8.1.13/ext/mysqli/configure

In work/php-8.1.13/ext/mysqli/configure there is this, which I guess should be able to find the socket:

  for i in  \
    /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock \
    /var/tmp/mysql.sock \
    /var/run/mysql/mysql.sock \
    /var/lib/mysql/mysql.sock \
    /var/mysql/mysql.sock \
    /usr/local/mysql/var/mysql.sock \
    /Private/tmp/mysql.sock \
    /private/tmp/mysql.sock \
    /tmp/mysql.sock \
  ; do
    if test -r $i; then
      MYSQL_SOCK=$i
      break 2
    fi
  done

Maybe somebody with more knowledge about this is able to help out.
